title: Colour palettes
text: Colours can frustrating get just right R. palr package provides simple palette functions standard colour schemes matched real data values. three main ways working palr palette functions. pal(n) return n colours palette pal(data) return right colour values data pal(palette = TRUE) return entire palette, colours cols intervals breaks

title: Colour palettes
text: show examples use palr functions. oisst data set subset NOAA 1/4° daily Optimum Interpolation Sea Surface Temperature (Reynolds, 2007) obtained National Oceanic Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).  default plot colours uses setting provided base package, SST data degrees Celsius can use sst_pal function give specific colours particular temperatures. full range temperatures shown plot legend, even though data values range NA, NA.  palette colours data absolute palette can also plot data correctly scale.
code: library(palr) data(oisst) image(oisst) sstcols <- sst_pal(palette = TRUE) image(oisst, col = sstcols$col, zlim = range(sstcols$breaks))

title: Colour palettes
text: function image_pal() can used bake particular colour scheme data. bit like colourvalues package function colour_values(), takes raw values maps colour scale modelled image() function. image function takes set colours set breaks define colour scale, andimage_pal()` mirrors defaults.  using breaks able control actual scale colour mapping, can provide different data values still get colour value input (set scale based original data might wrong range). ’s simpler interface using absolute colours specifying every break, using zlim. value ignored breaks set.   analogous worker functions image_raster() image_stars() packages raster stars, can emulate given plot effect save data object, can easily written image format GeoTIFF PNG.
code: (col <- image_pal(1:10, breaks = c(0, 4, 8, 9, 10), col = c("black", "green", "red", "blue"))) ##  [1] "#000000" "#000000" "#000000" "#000000" "#00FF00" "#00FF00" "#00FF00" ##  [8] "#00FF00" "#FF0000" "#0000FF" plot(1:10, col = col, pch = 19) image(volcano, zlim = c(130, max(volcano))) vals <- sort(unique(volcano)) cols <- image_pal(vals, zlim = c(130, max(volcano))) plot(vals, col = cols); abline(h = 130)

title: Colour Palettes for Data
text: palr provides colour palettes data, based well known remotely sensed data sets sea ice concentration, sea surface temperature chlorophyll-. includes function image_pal() create data used image() function colours. five main ways working palr palette functions, names like ice_pal(), chl_pal() sst_pal() ice, chlorophyll-sst respectively mk_timePal creating time-mapped palette function. ice_pal(n) return n colours palette ice_pal(data) return right colour values data ice_pal(palette = TRUE) returns entire palette, colours cols intervals breaks tfun <- mk_timePal(datetimes, pal); plot(x, tfun(dt)) match date-times palette, palette function. image_pal(data, col = colors, breaks = breaks) emulates behaviour image() return colours data rather draw plot d_pal(data) returns colour set values See website documentation: https://australianantarcticdivision.github.io/palr/

title: Colour Palettes for Data
text: Install current release CRAN using install development version: Please note palr project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.
code: install.packages("palr") library(devtools) install_github("AustralianAntarcticDivision/palr")

code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("raster")) { nsidcfile <- system.file("extdata", "nt_20140320_f17_v01_s.bin",      package = "palr", mustWork = TRUE) r <- raster::raster(nsidcfile)  icp <- ice_pal(palette = TRUE) ## The AMSR colours raster::plot(r, col = icp$col, zlim = range(icp$breaks), main = sprintf("NSIDC ice %s", format(raster::getZ(r)))) } #> Loading required namespace: raster  # }

what: Convert image data to a matrix of hex colours — image_hex
title: Convert image data to a matrix of hex colours — image_hex
text: `image_hex()` turns numeric, integer, raw, logical, character image data character matrix hex colours, ready [graphics::rasterImage()], writing PNG/GeoTIFF, use texture. colour engine shared 'ximage' package.

title: Convert image data to a matrix of hex colours — image_hex
code: image_hex(   x,   col = NULL,   breaks = NULL,   zlim = NULL,   alpha = NULL,   na.col = "transparent" )

title: Convert image data to a matrix of hex colours — image_hex
text: x matrix array image data (see Details) col colours map , vector function breaks numeric breakpoints colours zlim absolute data range palette (single-band ) alpha opacity multiplier [0, 1] na.col colour missing values (default "transparent")

title: Convert image data to a matrix of hex colours — image_hex
text: character matrix hex colours

title: Convert image data to a matrix of hex colours — image_hex
text: Supported inputs matrix (palette mapping via 'col', 'breaks', 'zlim'), 3D array 1 band (treated matrix), 2 bands (grey + alpha), 3 bands (RGB), 4 bands (RGBA), raw matrix array (converted integer), character matrix colours (passed , NA replaced 'na.col'). Behaviour guarantees: Non-finite values (NA, NaN, Inf) always map 'na.col'. Colour scaling multi-band data autodetected per image: data within [0, 1] used -, within [0, 255] divided 255, anything else rescaled finite range colour bands jointly (hue relationships preserved); alpha band scaled independently. Constant (zero-range) data maps middle palette. 'zlim' anchors palette absolute range values outside map 'na.col'; 'zlim' ignored warning multi-band input. 'alpha' constant (recycled) opacity multiplier [0, 1] applied top existing alpha. Use stretch functions ([stretch_linear()] friends) normalize high dynamic range data colour mapping.

title: Convert image data to a matrix of hex colours — image_hex
code: m <- image_hex(volcano) dim(m) #> [1] 87 61 ## rgb array in 0, 255 arr <- array(c(volcano, volcano / 2, 255 - volcano), c(dim(volcano), 3L)) m <- image_hex(arr) ## plot it plot(NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), asp = 1) rasterImage(image_hex(volcano, col = grDevices::hcl.colors(64)), 0, 0, 1, 1)

title: Map data values to colours — image_pal
text: x numeric values, raster object (single layer ) stars object (single variable, 2D array ) col function generate colours, vector hex colours ... ignored breaks optionally used specify colour mapping n optionally used specify density colours `col` (ignored `breaks` set) zlim numeric range clamp values absolute scale (ignored `breaks` set)

title: Map data values to colours — image_pal
text: `image_pal()` vector hex colours, `image_raster` `image_stars` raster stars object 3 channel RGB (range 0,255)

title: Map data values to colours — image_pal
text: function `image_pal()` returns hex character string colours. function `image_raster()` map raster numeric values RGB 3-layer (channel) raster brick, `image_stars()` similarly 3-dimensional stars object. Please note expansion 3-channels fairly wasteful thing , overall data expanded single layer three faciliates specific task creating textures 3D mapping, way currently. also useful situations, controlling exactly kind plots can achieve exporting image formats 'GeoTIFF' 'PNG'.

title: Map data values to colours — image_pal
code: vals <- sort(rnorm(100)) cols <- image_pal(vals, zlim = c(-2.4, .5)) plot(vals, col = cols); abline(h = .5) points(vals, pch  = ".") ## zlim excluded some of the range

title: Time-indexed colour. — mk_timePal
text: Create time-indexed colour map, useful maintaining absolute scale across time series function date-time.

title: Time-indexed colour. — mk_timePal
code: mk_timePal(x, col)

title: Time-indexed colour. — mk_timePal
text: x date-times col colours, can function actual set colours

title: Time-indexed colour. — mk_timePal
text: function date-time

title: Time-indexed colour. — mk_timePal
code: dts <- seq(as.Date("1749-01-01"), by = "1 month", length.out = length(sunspots)) d <- data.frame(date = dts, sunspots = as.vector(t(sunspots))) tpal <- mk_timePal(d$date, col = sst_pal(50)) par(mfrow  = c(2, 1)) plot(sunspots ~ date, col = tpal(date), data = d) ## colours maintained by absolute date plot(sunspots ~ date, col = tpal(date), data = d[1500:1800, ], cex = 2) ## we can now insert new points and maintain this colour ramp d2 <- data.frame(date = seq(min(d$date), max(d$date), by = "5 days")) d2$sunspots <- approxfun(d$date, d$sunspots)(d2$date) points(sunspots ~ date, col = tpal(date), data = d2, pch = 19, cex = 0.5)

title: Stretch numeric values to [0, 1] — stretch_linear
text: family contrast-stretch functions rescale numeric vectors, matrices, arrays unit interval. data-normalization step naturally precedes colour mapping [d_pal()], [image_pal()], [image_hex()]. Dimensions input preserved, stretched matrix can go straight image plot.

title: Stretch numeric values to [0, 1] — stretch_linear
code: stretch_linear(x, low = 0.02, high = 0.98, lim = NULL)  stretch_log(x, low = 0.02, high = 0.98, offset = NULL, lim = NULL)  stretch_sqrt(x, low = 0.02, high = 0.98, offset = NULL, lim = NULL)  stretch_histeq(x, n = 256)

title: Stretch numeric values to [0, 1] — stretch_linear
text: x numeric vector, matrix, array (NA values preserved) low, high quantile probabilities clip bounds (default 2nd 98th percentiles), ignored 'lim' set lim optional absolute clip bounds data units, range like 'c(0, 3000)' (`stretch_log()` `stretch_sqrt()` given original data scale transformed internally) offset numeric offset added log sqrt transform. `NULL` (default), auto-detected values non-negative. n number bins histogram equalisation (default 256)

title: Stretch numeric values to [0, 1] — stretch_linear
text: numeric vector, matrix, array matching input, values  [0, 1] (NA input finite)

title: Stretch numeric values to [0, 1] — stretch_linear
text: `stretch_linear()` clips quantile bounds linearly rescales, workhorse default. `stretch_log()` applies `log1p` shifting values non-negative, linearly rescales. Useful data high dynamic range (e.g. overview-resolution satellite imagery integer counts span several orders magnitude). `stretch_sqrt()` applies square-root shifting, gentler compression log, good middle ground. `stretch_histeq()` maps values empirical quantile rank, producing approximately uniform distribution. Good scenes detail deep shadow bright areas simultaneously. default clip bounds quantiles data , result scene-dependent. Use 'lim' give absolute bounds data units instead (example 'lim = c(0, 3000)' Sentinel-2 surface reflectance), fixes mapping stable across tiles timesteps (seams mosaics, flicker animations). 'lim' set quantile arguments ignored. Values stretch outside [0, 1] clamped. Non-finite input values (NA, NaN) returned NA; constant input maps 0.5. multi-band data (e.g. RGB), apply per-band maximum per-channel contrast, pool bands one vector joint stretch preserves inter-band luminance relationships.

title: Stretch numeric values to [0, 1] — stretch_linear
text: worked examples sentinel2_stretch

title: Stretch numeric values to [0, 1] — stretch_linear
code: x <- sort(rnorm(200)) plot(x, stretch_linear(x), pch = ".")  plot(x, stretch_sqrt(x), pch = ".")   ## high dynamic range data benefits from log stretch x <- c(rexp(500, 0.01), rexp(500, 1)) plot(x, stretch_linear(x), pch = ".", main = "linear")  plot(x, stretch_log(x), pch = ".", main = "log")   ## histogram equalisation plot(x, stretch_histeq(x), pch = ".", main = "histeq")   ## absolute bounds, stable across scenes stretch_linear(c(-100, 0, 1500, 3000, 9000), lim = c(0, 3000)) #> [1] 0.0 0.0 0.5 1.0 1.0  ## feed into colour mapping plot(1:200, col = d_pal(stretch_linear(sort(rnorm(200)))), pch = 19)

title: palr 0.5.0
text: Fixed long-standing bug sst_pal(), chl_pal(), ice_pal(), bathy_deep_pal(): data values first palette break silently dropped output (zero index findInterval()), shortening result misaligning colours. range values now clamp end colours output length always matches input length. absolute palettes now share one internal worker abs_pal(), unifying alpha handling n-colours/data/palette/function modes. function mode (pal()) now respects alpha. New function image_hex() converts matrices arrays (1-band palette, grey+alpha, RGB, RGBA, raw, logical, character) matrix hex colours. colour engine shared {ximage} package, robust handling missing, non-finite, constant data. New stretch functions stretch_linear(), stretch_log(), stretch_sqrt(), stretch_histeq() rescale data [0, 1] ahead colour mapping, based experience Sentinel-2. preserve matrix/array dimensions, map constant input 0.5, return NA non-finite input, accept absolute bounds via lim scene-independent stretches. New function dirty_pal() global bathymetry/topography (AAD underway, DiRT). Follows standard palr palette modes. d_pal() image_pal() now map constant data mid-palette colour instead returning NA. Tests moved testthat edition 3, fixed test used magrittr pipe without available. Removed unused Suggests viridis, removed large blocks commented-legacy code.

title: palr 0.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-11-06 ice_pal() now defaults pleasing NSIDC blue palette (use amsre = TRUE get old behavour). Fixed problem missing values d_pal(). # palr 0.3.0 New function d_pal() alias data_pal() colour data values (like {colourvalues} simpler). Removed ftp links thanks CRAN.

title: palr 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-01-30 Remove need sp package converting example data matrix.

title: palr 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2019-11-07 Behaviour image_pal() tweaked match graphics::image.default() closely. New alias functions bathy_deep_pal() (bathyDeepPal), chl_pal() (chlPal), ice_pal() (icePal), sst_pal() (sstPal) start move consistent better names. old function names soft deprecated. New function image_pal() return colours created image() numeric input. Includes partner functions image_raster() image_stars() create objects raster stars format. packages now included Suggests. Converted NEWS NEWS.md.
